summary.intent: Fix a series of review findings on PR #691 (fogodev/ars-ui) related to navigation menu controlled-state behavior, then get the PR through CI and Codex review approval.
summary.outcome: Three rounds of fixes were applied and pushed: controlled-lag focus behavior, direction preservation across prop syncs, SetItems close decision logic, open_item_plan reconvergence, and a final empty-registry edge case. All CI checks passed and all Codex threads were resolved.

repo: The repo uses Codex as an automated code reviewer triggered via '@codex review' PR comment; a thumbs-up/eyes reaction signals in-progress vs. approved.
CI pipeline has a sequenced gate structure: fmt/snapshot first, then clippy/workspace, then unit tests, then adapter/browser/feature/coverage/release checks.

Navigation menu uses a bindable value pattern where 'rendered value' (bindable) can lag 'internal state', and close/open decisions must be based on the rendered value to handle controlled-state lag correctly.
open_item() in an empty registry falls back rather than returning None — SetItems([]) did not previously force-clear an active value, leaving the menu logically open.
Co-author attribution for Codex is done via 'Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>' in commit messages.

code.finding: SetItems reconciliation logic: originally skipped empty registries, allowing open_item() to fallback. Fixed so explicit empty SetItems clears an active value.
open_item_plan: previously no-oped when internal state matched, missing reconvergence case during controlled lag. Fixed to no-op only when both rendered value and internal state already match.
SyncProps was rewriting ctx.dir, overwriting adapter-resolved Auto direction. Fixed so dir prop changes emit SetDirection explicitly instead.

workflow: After each push, all Codex review threads must be manually resolved via GraphQL mutation before triggering a new '@codex review' pass.
cargo llvm-cov used for coverage gating on the navigation_menu module specifically.
cargo +nightly fmt --all required for formatting; drift can appear even on non-modified files and must be applied before committing.
Polling CI via 'gh pr checks' with sleep intervals is the primary monitoring strategy; job-level detail available via 'gh api repos/.../actions/jobs/{id}'.

friction: Long CI pipeline with sequential gates means multiple polling cycles (30s, 45s, 60s, 90s, 180s intervals) before all checks complete.
Codex review reaction (eyes) does not give ETA; had to poll GraphQL for new comment threads to detect when review results were posted.
rustfmt drift on unrelated lines required a separate fmt pass between test runs, adding an extra round-trip.
GraphQL thread resolution requires fetching thread IDs first, then issuing individual mutations per thread — no batch resolve API.
open_items: Codex third-pass review result not yet confirmed as approved at session end — PR may need another '@codex review' trigger if new findings emerge.
Coverage/release/adapter/browser CI checks were still running at the point the empty-registry fix was pushed — final green status not confirmed in transcript.
The empty-registry fix adds a regression test but broader controlled-state lag scenarios may still have edge cases not covered by the current test suite.
